meals.name: Kid-Friendly Honey Garlic Chicken Stir Fry
meals.description: A sweet and savory chicken stir fry packed with colorful vegetables, served over fluffy white rice. Total cook time is approximately 25 minutes.

ingredients.name: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Heritage Farm® Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs

ingredients.name: Fresh ginger
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Evolution Fresh® Organic Citrus Ginger Zest Cold-Pressed Vegetable and Fruit Juice Blend

ingredients.name: Cornstarch
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Comforts™ Pure Cornstarch Baby Powder With Aloe Vera & Vitamin E

ingredients.name: Broccoli florets
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Birds Eye Baby Broccoli Florets, Frozen Vegetables

ingredients.name: Yellow onion
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Simple Truth Organic® Yellow Onion 2 lb Bag

ingredients.name: Long-grain white rice
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Mahatma Extra Long Grain White Rice, Uncooked Rice, 80 oz (2.27kg)

instructions: Step 1: Cook the rice. Rinse 1.5 cups of long-grain white rice until water runs clear. Combine with 3 cups water and a pinch of salt in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-18 minutes until water is absorbed. Remove from heat and let stand, covered, for 5 minutes.
Step 2: Prepare chicken and sauce. Cut chicken thighs into 1-inch pieces. In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, honey, minced garlic, grated ginger, and cornstarch. Set aside.

Step 3: Stir-fry chicken and vegetables. Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken from skillet and set aside.

Step 4: Cook vegetables. Add another 1 tablespoon olive oil to the skillet. Add broccoli, carrots, sliced yellow onion, and red bell pepper. Stir-fry for 5-7 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp. Return chicken to the skillet.

Step 5: Combine and serve. Pour the prepared sauce over the chicken and vegetables. Stir constantly for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats everything. Drizzle with sesame oil and sprinkle with sesame seeds before serving immediately over the cooked rice.

meals.name: Quick Skillet Beef Tacos with Creamy Queso
meals.description: Fast and flavorful ground beef tacos seasoned to perfection, topped with fresh veggies and pantry-staple sour cream and queso. Total cook time is approximately 20 minutes.

ingredients.name: Ground beef
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Private Selection® 80/20 Angus Ground Beef Tray 1 LB

ingredients.name: Taco seasoning mix
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Old El Paso™ Original Flavor Taco Seasoning Mix

ingredients.name: Shredded lettuce
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Kroger® Iceberg Shredded Lettuce Bagged Salad BIG DEAL!

instructions: Step 1: Brown the ground beef. Heat 1 teaspoon olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground beef and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until no longer pink, about 6-8 minutes. Drain any excess grease.
Step 2: Season the beef. Stir in the taco seasoning mix and 1/4 cup water. Bring to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and coated the beef.

Step 3: Prepare toppings and tortillas. While the beef is simmering, warm the flour tortillas according to package instructions (e.g., in a microwave, oven, or dry skillet). Dice tomatoes and finely dice red onion; shred lettuce.

Step 4: Assemble the tacos. Spoon the seasoned ground beef into the warm tortillas. Top generously with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, diced red onion, a dollop of sour cream, and a drizzle of salsa con queso. Serve with lime wedges for squeezing.

meals.name: One-Pan Sausage & Egg Hash with Smoked Paprika
meals.description: A quick and easy sheet pan meal featuring savory sausage, crispy potatoes, and tender-crisp vegetables, topped with baked eggs. Total time under 30 minutes.

ingredients.name: Yellow onion
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Simple Truth Organic® Yellow Onion 2 lb Bag

ingredients.name: Dried thyme
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Litehouse™ Instantly Fresh Freeze Dried Thyme

instructions: Step 1: Preheat oven and prepare vegetables. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). On a large baking sheet, toss the diced potatoes, chopped yellow onion, and chopped green bell pepper with 2 tablespoons olive oil, smoked paprika, dried thyme, salt, and pepper. Spread in a single layer.

Step 2: Roast sausage and vegetables. Add the sliced or crumbled breakfast sausage to the baking sheet, distributing it evenly among the vegetables. Roast for 15-20 minutes, stirring halfway through, until potatoes are tender and sausage is cooked through.

Step 3: Add eggs. Once the sausage and vegetables are cooked, make four small wells in the mixture on the baking sheet. Crack one egg into each well. Return the baking sheet to the oven and bake for an additional 8-10 minutes, or until egg whites are set and yolks are to your desired runniness.
Step 4: Garnish and serve. Carefully remove the baking sheet from the oven. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley and serve immediately.

meals.name: Garlic Honey Pork and Broccoli Rice Bowls
meals.description: Tender pork tenderloin and crisp broccoli coated in a savory garlic-honey glaze, served over rice for a delicious bowl-style meal. Total cook time is approximately 25 minutes.

ingredients.name: Pork tenderloin
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Kroger® Seasoned and Seared Pre-Sliced Pork Tenderloin

ingredients.name: Fresh ginger
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Evolution Fresh® Organic Citrus Ginger Zest Cold-Pressed Vegetable and Fruit Juice Blend

ingredients.name: Cornstarch
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Comforts™ Pure Cornstarch Baby Powder With Aloe Vera & Vitamin E

ingredients.name: Broccoli florets
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Birds Eye Baby Broccoli Florets, Frozen Vegetables

instructions: Step 1: Prepare the sauce. In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, honey, minced garlic, grated ginger, and cornstarch. Set aside.

Step 2: Cook the pork. Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add pork pieces and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 6-8 minutes. Remove pork from skillet and set aside.

Step 3: Cook broccoli. Add another 1 tablespoon olive oil to the skillet. Add broccoli florets and stir-fry for 5-7 minutes until tender-crisp. You can add a tablespoon of water and cover for a minute to steam them lightly if preferred.

Step 4: Combine and serve. Return the cooked pork to the skillet with the broccoli. Pour the prepared sauce over the pork and broccoli. Stir constantly for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats everything. Serve immediately over cooked long-grain white rice, garnished with sliced green onions.

meals.name: Baked Lemon Herb Salmon with Roasted Asparagus
meals.description: Flaky salmon fillets baked with fresh herbs and lemon alongside tender roasted asparagus, a light and healthy meal. Total cook time is approximately 25 minutes.

ingredients.name: Salmon fillets
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Great American Seafood Farm Raised Atlantic Salmon Fillets

ingredients.name: Asparagus spears
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Birds Eye Steamfresh Asparagus Spears, Frozen Vegetables

ingredients.name: Fresh dill
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Oh Snap!® Dilly Bites® Fresh Dill Pickle Snack Pack

instructions: Step 1: Preheat oven and prepare asparagus. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). On a large baking sheet, toss the trimmed asparagus with 1 tablespoon olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread in a single layer.

Step 2: Prepare salmon. Pat salmon fillets dry. In a small bowl, whisk together 2 tablespoons olive oil, the juice from half a lemon, chopped fresh dill, and minced garlic. Season salmon fillets with salt and pepper.

Step 3: Bake salmon and asparagus. Place the seasoned salmon fillets on the same baking sheet as the asparagus, pushing asparagus to one side if needed. Spoon or brush the lemon-herb mixture over the salmon. Top each fillet with a lemon slice from the remaining half.

Step 4: Cook and serve. Bake for 12-18 minutes, depending on the thickness of the salmon and desired doneness. Salmon should flake easily with a fork and reach an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C). Serve immediately.

meals.name: Slow Cooker BBQ Pulled Beef Sandwiches with Creamy Coleslaw
meals.description: Tender, flavorful pulled beef cooked low and slow in a tangy BBQ sauce, served on toasted buns with a crisp, creamy coleslaw. Total cook time is approximately 6-8 hours.

ingredients.name: Boneless beef chuck roast
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Tyson Premium Quality Savory Homestyle Beef Chuck Roast

ingredients.name: Yellow onion
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Simple Truth Organic® Yellow Onion 2 lb Bag

ingredients.name: Apple cider vinegar
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Simple Truth Organic™ Organic Raw Apple Cider Vinegar

ingredients.name: Brown sugar
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Simple Truth Organic® Light Brown Sugar

ingredients.name: Worcestershire sauce
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Lea & Perrins The Original Worcestershire Sauce

ingredients.name: Hamburger buns
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Kroger® Rich N Honey Hamburger Buns

instructions: Step 1: Sear beef (optional but recommended). Season beef chuck roast generously with salt and pepper. Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear beef on all sides until browned, about 3-4 minutes per side. Transfer beef to the slow cooker.

Step 2: Prepare slow cooker. Add sliced yellow onion and minced garlic around the beef in the slow cooker. In a medium bowl, whisk together BBQ sauce, apple cider vinegar, brown sugar, and Worcestershire sauce. Pour the sauce over the beef.

Step 3: Slow cook. Cover the slow cooker and cook on low for 6-8 hours, or on high for 3-4 hours, until the beef is fork-tender and easily shreds apart. Internal temperature should be 200-205°F (93-96°C).

Step 4: Make coleslaw and assemble. While beef is cooking, prepare coleslaw by combining shredded cabbage and carrots in a large bowl. In a separate small bowl, whisk together mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, sugar, salt, and pepper. Pour over cabbage mixture and toss to combine. When beef is done, remove from slow cooker, shred with two forks, and return to the sauce to coat. Serve the pulled beef on toasted hamburger buns with a side of creamy coleslaw.

meals.name: Hearty Homemade Chicken and Vegetable Pot Pie
meals.description: A comforting pot pie filled with tender chicken and mixed vegetables in a creamy, savory sauce, all topped with a flaky, golden crust. Total cook time is approximately 60 minutes.

ingredients.name: Boneless, skinless chicken breasts
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Heritage Farm® Boneless Skinless Chicken Breasts

ingredients.name: Yellow onion
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Simple Truth Organic® Yellow Onion 2 lb Bag

ingredients.name: Celery stalks
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Kroger® Carrots Celery And Broccoli Snack Tray With Ranch Dip

ingredients.name: Dried thyme
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Litehouse™ Instantly Fresh Freeze Dried Thyme

ingredients.name: Pie crusts
ingredients.amount: 2 store-bought, or homemade with 2.5 cups flour and 1 cup butter
ingredients.kroger_product_name: Kroger® Unroll and Bake Pie Crusts

instructions: Step 1: Prepare chicken and preheat oven. Cook chicken breasts (e.g., by boiling or baking) until an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) is reached, then shred. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).

Step 2: Make the filling base. In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt 1/2 cup butter over medium heat. Add chopped yellow onion, diced carrots, and diced celery. Cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are tender, about 8-10 minutes. Stir in 1/2 cup flour and cook for 1 minute, creating a roux.

Step 3: Add liquids and seasoning. Gradually whisk in chicken broth and whole milk until smooth. Bring to a simmer, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in dried thyme, shredded chicken, frozen peas, salt, and pepper. Remove from heat.

Step 4: Assemble and bake the pie. Pour the chicken and vegetable filling into a 9-inch pie dish. If using store-bought crusts, place one crust over the filling, trim edges, and crimp. Cut a few slits in the top for venting. Brush with beaten egg wash. If making from scratch, prepare a simple pie dough with 2.5 cups flour, 1 cup cold butter, and ice water, then roll out and place over filling.
Step 5: Bake. Bake for 30-40 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is bubbly. If the crust browns too quickly, loosely tent with foil. Let stand for 10 minutes before serving.
